panels.description: GenAI platform availability SLO - Target 99.5% for 28day window

targets.lsql:
HTTP

| kubernetes.container.name := #kubernetes.container.name

| drop(#kubernetes.container.name) //aks repository restructure - line can be deleted after 2025-06-01 

| kubernetes.container.name=/$container/i

| #kubernetes.namespace=/$namespace/i

| #kubernetes.cluster=/$cluster/i

| kubernetes.pod_labels.release=$release

// Step 1: Extract the 3-digit status code from the log message
| regex(field=message, "\"\\s.*?(?P<status_code>\\d{3})")

// Step 2: Flag requests with 5xx and 429 status codes as errors
| is_5xx_error := if(regex(field=status_code, "(5..|429)"), then=1, else=0)

// Step 3: Aggregate the total count of requests and the total count of 5xx errors
| [sum(is_5xx_error, as=error_count), count(as=total_count)]

| slo_metric := if(total_count > 0, then=(((total_count - error_count) / total_count) * 100), else=100)

// Step 5: Display the final SLO metric
| select(slo_metric)

panels.title: SLO Availability (5xx,429)

panels.description:

for 28 day window

targets.lsql:
HTTP

| kubernetes.container.name := #kubernetes.container.name

| drop(#kubernetes.container.name) //aks repository restructure - line can be deleted after 2025-06-01 

| kubernetes.container.name=/$container/i

| #kubernetes.namespace=/$namespace/i

| #kubernetes.cluster=/$cluster/i

| kubernetes.pod_labels.release=$release

// Step 1: Extract the 3-digit status code from the log message
| regex(field=message, "\"\\s.*?(?P<status_code>\\d{3})")

// Step 2: Flag requests with 4xx status codes as errors
| is_4xx_error := if(regex(field=status_code, "4(?!29)\\d{2}"), then=1, else=0)

// Step 3: Aggregate the total count of requests and the total count of 4xx errors
| [sum(is_4xx_error, as=error_count), count(as=total_count)]

| slo_metric := if(total_count > 0, then=(((total_count - error_count) / total_count) * 100), else=100)

// Step 5: Display the final SLO metric
| select(slo_metric)

for 28 day window

targets.lsql:
"execution statistics:" AND "openAI"

| kubernetes.container.name := #kubernetes.container.name

| drop(#kubernetes.container.name) //aks repository restructure - line can be deleted after 2025-06-01 

| kubernetes.container.name=/$container/i

| #kubernetes.namespace=/$namespace/i

| #kubernetes.cluster=/$cluster/i

| kubernetes.pod_labels.release=$release

| regex(field=message, "execution statistics: {(?:.*?'redis':\\s*(?<redis>\\d+\\.\\d+))?(?:.*?'redis\\.rate_limit':\\s*(?<redis_rate_limit>\\d+\\.\\d+))?(?:.*?'openAI':\\s*(?<openAI>\\d+\\.\\d+))?(?:.*?'cosmosDB':\\s*(?<cosmosDB>\\d+\\.\\d+))?(?:.*?'cosmosDB\\.logging_prompt':\\s*(?<cosmosDB_logging_prompt>\\d+\\.\\d+))?(?:.*?'cosmosDB\\.logging_consumption':\\s*(?<cosmosDB_logging_consumption>\\d+\\.\\d+))?(?:.*?'redis\\.logging_consumption':\\s*(?<redis_logging_consumption>\\d+\\.\\d+))?.*}")

// Step 3: Sum the component latencies. Non-existent fields are treated as 0.
| total_component_latency := redis  + openAI + cosmosDB

// Step 4: Calculate the 95th percentile of the total calculated latency.
| percentile(field=total_component_latency, percentiles=[95], as=latency)

// Step 5: Assign the result to a clearly named field and display it.
| p95_latency_sec := latency_95
| select(p95_latency_sec)

// Step 3: Sum the component latencies. Non-existent fields are treated as 0.

| select(p95_latency_sec)

targets.lsql:
// Step 1: Filter for logs that represent HTTP requests using a fast free-text search.
"HTTP" AND not livez
| kubernetes.container.name := #kubernetes.container.name

| drop(#kubernetes.container.name) //aks repository restructure - line can be deleted after 2025-06-01 

| kubernetes.container.name=/$container/i

| #kubernetes.namespace=/$namespace/i

| #kubernetes.cluster=/$cluster/i

| kubernetes.pod_labels.release=$release

// Step 2: Create a time series chart that counts the events in 1-second buckets.
| timechart(span=1s, function=count(as=all_requests_per_second))

// Step 1: Filter for logs that represent HTTP requests using a fast free-text search.
"HTTP" AND not livez AND openai
| kubernetes.container.name := #kubernetes.container.name

| drop(#kubernetes.container.name) //aks repository restructure - line can be deleted after 2025-06-01 

| kubernetes.container.name=/$container/i

| #kubernetes.namespace=/$namespace/i

| #kubernetes.cluster=/$cluster/i

// Step 2: Create a time series chart that counts the events in 1-second buckets.
| timechart(span=1s, function=count(as=openai_requests_per_second))

// Step 1: Filter for logs that represent HTTP requests using a fast free-text search.
"HTTP" AND not livez AND vertexai
| kubernetes.container.name := #kubernetes.container.name

| drop(#kubernetes.container.name) //aks repository restructure - line can be deleted after 2025-06-01 

| kubernetes.container.name=/$container/i

| #kubernetes.namespace=/$namespace/i

| #kubernetes.cluster=/$cluster/i

// Step 2: Create a time series chart that counts the events in 1-second buckets.
| timechart(span=1s, function=count(as=vertexai_requests_per_second))
